name: Nimba County (Liberia)
description: White field with the Liberian national flag as a canton in the upper hoist; in the fly half a green-and-brown mountain landscape under a pale-blue sky with an orange sun represents Mount Nimba, the iron-ore peak that gives the county its name.
history: Presented by President William V. S. Tubman on 29 November 1965 together with the flags of the other Liberian counties.

features.symbolism: Nimba as part of the Republic of Liberia
Mount Nimba — the iron-ore-rich mountain massif from which the county takes its name
